transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Good afternoon everyone. End of Sprint 4 Week 1. I want a full honest account of what happened this week — what went well, what went wrong, and what the plan is for Week 2. This week was not smooth and I want us to understand exactly why, so we don't repeat it. Rupali, take us through it.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: I'll give it chronologically. Monday morning — staging environment was still down. Vivek had done everything on his side before the sprint started, the infra ticket was raised Friday. But central infra hadn't applied the config as of Monday 9am. Vivek escalated during the standup itself, infra applied it at 1:20pm. So we lost Monday morning — about 4 hours on Day 1 of a sprint where we had a Wednesday go-live target.

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: I want to be clear on the record — the ticket was raised last Friday before the sprint started, with Vinod sir's name as escalation. The delay was entirely on central infra's side. I was chasing them from 8am Monday.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: Understood Vivek, that's noted. Once the environment came up Monday afternoon, Varsha caught something critical — we were using the wrong payment gateway endpoint. Client had sent two URLs in their integration doc and we had the wrong one configured. Varsha fixed it Tuesday morning and shared the corrected API response schema with Suraj.

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: Yes. And I also found a timeout configuration issue — we had the gateway timeout set to 3 seconds but the integration doc says their first authentication call can take up to 8 seconds. That was causing roughly 1 in 5 payment initiation calls to fail. Ajay was seeing this in testing Tuesday night and messaged the group at midnight. We changed it to 10 seconds and added retry logic Tuesday morning.

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: I ran the test suite from 2pm Tuesday, once the env and endpoint were fixed. The 20% timeout failure rate was clear and I kept going until late to understand the pattern — it was happening specifically on the first call of a session. After Varsha's timeout fix and the retry logic, that went away. By Wednesday morning we had a working happy path.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Now tell me about the Wednesday demo.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: The Wednesday demo failed, sir. Client saw an error screen during the payment flow. The root cause — Varsha's fixes were deployed to staging-v2. Ajay had run the tests on staging-v2 and confirmed they were passing. But the demo environment the client was looking at was staging-v1. The fix had not reached that environment. It was a coordination gap, not a code issue, but client didn't distinguish between the two.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: That's on me. I should have verified which environment the client was using before we started the demo. I assumed it was v2. It wasn't. We've fixed this — Vivek built a single deployment pipeline that pushes to both staging-v1 and staging-v2 simultaneously. It's been live since Wednesday noon. That specific failure cannot recur.

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: Correct. Single pipeline, both environments always in sync from now on. I confirmed it with a test deployment Wednesday afternoon.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: What happened Thursday?

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: Thursday morning Vivek found something serious. The SSL certificate authority on prod is different from staging. Central infra set up prod with a proper CA-signed cert. Staging still has a self-signed cert. Our payment API calls were going to fail the moment we hit prod. Vivek had actually flagged this exact issue two weeks ago in an infra sync meeting. It wasn't acted on.

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: I raised it Thursday morning standup and immediately opened a formal infra ticket with escalation. Infra resolved it at 11:15pm Thursday night. The moment it was fixed, Ajay started running the full test suite on prod.

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: I ran on prod from 11:30pm last night till 1:30am. Results — 30 of 34 passing on prod. 4 failing. 2 are the gateway-down async fallback scenarios we've agreed to descope. The other 2 are intermittent — they pass sometimes and fail sometimes. Running them 5 times, they failed roughly 3 out of 5. My assessment — it's load-related, not a code bug. Gateway response time under real network conditions occasionally pushes past our 10-second timeout.

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: That's my read too. The fix is the async retry queue we descoped earlier in the week. With that queue, a timeout doesn't mean a failed transaction — it queues and retries. Without it, the failure surfaces to the user. We're running on prod without that safety net.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: We went with a partial go-live today. I drafted the communication to client — I called it Phase 1 go-live. We clearly documented the 2 intermittent cases and the descoped async queue, with a hotfix ETA. Client acknowledged it and Vinod sir reviewed the communication before it went out.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: That's the honest situation. For Week 2 — what's the priority list?

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: Week 2 has three priorities. First — the async retry queue. That's what fixes the 2 intermittent prod failures and closes the gateway-down descoped scenario. Varsha estimates 2-3 days for a proper implementation. Second — get 34 of 34 tests passing on prod. Third — we have a Sprint 5 scope conversation with client that's getting complex. Client dropped inventory sync, returns flow, and admin dashboard all in one request. We need to manage that properly before we go into Sprint 5 planning.

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: On async queue — I need real queue infrastructure for testing. Redis or RabbitMQ. That's an infra dependency again. If we can get that provisioned early next week I can have the queue code done by Wednesday.

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: I'll flag it as an urgent infra request Monday morning. Given this week's experience, we should escalate it directly from Vinod sir rather than waiting for the normal ticket queue.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Agreed. Vivek and Rupali, come to me Monday with the Redis provisioning request and I'll send the mail myself. We're not losing another 2 days to infra delays. Ajay — I need 34 of 34 by end of next week. Not partial. Full.

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: Understood sir. That's the target I'm working to.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Team — this was a genuinely difficult week. Environment issues on Day 1, demo failure Day 3, SSL cert surprise Day 4. A lot went wrong. But 30 of 34 are passing on prod going into the weekend, the team was transparent about issues, and we have a clear plan for Week 2. That's not nothing. Varsha, Ajay — I heard you both worked late multiple nights this week. That doesn't go unnoticed. Let's make Week 2 cleaner.
